vocab.example: Learning to paint at a young age helped me develop a keen aesthetic appreciation for the world around me.

vocab.meaning: một phương thức giải tỏa sự sáng tạo
vocab.example: Drawing served as a vital creative outlet during my childhood, allowing me to express emotions I couldn't put into words.

vocab.meaning: vẽ nguệch ngoạc một cách vô định
vocab.example: I used to doodle aimlessly in the margins of my notebooks whenever I was bored in class.

vocab.meaning: nắm vững những nguyên tắc cơ bản
vocab.example: In my early art classes, I struggled to master the fundamentals of perspective and vanishing points.
Watercolors were my favorite artistic medium because of the way the colors blend so fluidly on paper.
I remember trying to paint landscapes that could evoke a sense of tranquility and peace.
As a child with a vivid imagination, I spent hours drawing mythical creatures and faraway galaxies.

vocab.meaning: sự chú ý tỉ mỉ đến từng chi tiết
vocab.example: My art teacher always praised my meticulous attention to detail when I was sketching still-life objects.
I experimented with bold brushstrokes to create texture, though my early attempts were quite messy.
I took private lessons under the guidance of a local artist who taught me the secrets of oil painting.
